persona.name: Jake Morrison

occupation.title: Sales Account Executive
occupation.description: Manages mid-market accounts for a data enrichment platform. Spends most of the day on Zoom calls and in Salesforce, travels to client sites quarterly. Competitive internally — tracks his numbers against peers on a public leaderboard.

persona.education: B.S. in Business Administration from Texas State University. Was a walk-on for the track team freshman year, didn't make the cut, switched to club running.
long_term_goals: Qualify for the Boston Marathon (targeting sub-3:00)
Complete a marathon on every continent
Save enough for a house in the Austin suburbs
persona.style: Jake talks fast and cuts to the point — sales trained him to respect people's time. He uses running jargon casually (splits, tempo pace, lactate threshold) and assumes everyone knows what a BQ is. He dresses like a runner even at work: running watch visible, sneakers at his standing desk. When excited about a product, he gets specific about numbers; when unimpressed, he goes quiet rather than argumentative.

traits: Competitive and goal-oriented in everything he does
Disciplined about training schedules but loose about everything else
Tracks metrics obsessively — pace, heart rate, cadence
Loyal to brands that perform but will switch without sentiment
Social runner — trains with a club but races alone

likes: Clear pace data on his Garmin
dislikes: Heavy shoes that slow him down
Shoes that lose responsiveness after 200 miles
Overly cushioned shoes that feel mushy
People who say running is bad for your knees
Paying full price when a previous model works fine

beliefs: The right shoe makes a measurable difference in race performance
Training consistency matters more than any single piece of gear
You earn a BQ — there are no shortcuts
Running is the best stress relief available
skills: Running pace calculation and race strategy

general: Logs every run on Strava immediately after finishing
Rotates between 3 pairs of running shoes based on workout type
Researches shoes for weeks before buying — reads reviews, watches YouTube comparisons
Wakes at 5:15 AM for weekday runs regardless of season

morning: 6-8 mile run with the club or solo
workday: Standing desk with running shoes on
Lunch at desk while watching running content
weekend: Grocery run and meal prep Sunday

health.physical: Strong cardiovascular fitness, low body fat. History of shin splints in college that resolved with proper shoes. Tight hamstrings that require daily stretching. Currently injury-free and in peak training.
health.mental: Generally positive and motivated. Gets irritable during taper weeks when mileage drops. Uses running as primary stress management — noticeably tense if he misses more than two days.

relationships.name: Chris Morrison
relationships.description: Also a runner but more casual — they argue about shoe brands at family dinners.

relationships.name: Tanya Osei
relationships.description: Yoga instructor who tolerates his running obsession. They compromise by doing partner workouts on Sundays.

relationships.name: Mark Delgado
relationships.description: They push each other on tempo days. Mark is slightly faster, which motivates Jake.

relationships.name: Lisa Morrison
relationships.description: Worries about his knees. Sends him articles about running injuries.
